LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT D. CAMERON BAKERS MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PWDF IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT WILL PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO REQUESTS NOS. 1-9 WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT IS WITHHOLDING ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENT BASED ON A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE, IT WILL DESCRIBE THE DOCUMENT WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY TO ENABLE PLAINTIFF TO CHALLENGE THE CLAIM. IN ADDITION, IF DEFENDANTS ASSERTS THAT IT IS UNABLE TO PRODUCE A DOCUMENT BASED UPON THE ORDER OF THE U.S. DISTRICT COURT IT SHALL DESCRIBE THAT DOCUMENT AND EXPLAIN WHY IT CONTENTS ARE COVERED BY THAT ORDER. THE COMPLAINT SEEKS MONEY DAMAGES FOR AN ALLEGED BREACH OF A CONTRACT TO PAY A BONUS IN LEGAL FEES BASED UPON RECOVERIES IN THE ACTIONS FILED IN THE U.S. DISTRICT COURT. ALL OF THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS RELATE TO THAT CLAIM AND SHOULD HAVE BEEN PRODUCED WITHOUT CONTROVERSY. DEFENDANT WAS NOT JUSTIFIED IN REFUSING TO PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENT WITHOUT A PROTECTIVE ORDER. MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE ASSESSED IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,000.00. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ROSE-ELLEN FAIRGRIEVE, ESQ. of Fairgrieve Law Office, Tel No. 415-890-6057 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Attys. TAD A. DEVLIN, ESQ. and SHEILA PHAM, ESQ. of Kaufman Dolowich & Voluck, LLP, Tel No. 415-926-7600 appearing for Defendant People With Disabilities Foundation.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF D BAKER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication is denied in its entirety. Baker is not entitled to judgment in his favor on his claim for breach of contract. The evidence establishes that Baker breached the contract on March 5, 2012, by resigning without giving two weeks notice. There are factual issues regarding whether PWDF disregarded the repudiation, whether Baker's continued performance retracted the repudiation, and PWDF's damages, if any. The Bruce declaration explains that PWDF needed to hire Vaca and incurred his expenses. Bruce also states that Baker's continued work was only transitional. Paragraph 3.2 of the contract is ambiguous regarding the bonus structure. The trier of fact needs to sort out the conflicting parol evidence. (Wolf v. Superior Court (2004) 114 Cal.App.4th 1343, 1351 ("[w]hen two equally plausible interpretations of the language of a contract may be made ... parol evidence is admissible to aid in interpreting the agreement, thereby presenting a question of fact which precludes summary judgment if the evidence is contradictory."). Baker's motion regarding PWDF's breach of contract claim is procedurally infirm. Edward Fineman Co. is off point. There, the court addressed whether the court could summarily adjudicate claims regarding 83 fraudulent checks. The court held that honoring each check was a distinct claim and summary adjudication was appropriate. Here, there is only one contract and granting summary adjudication as to a theory of liability violates CCP 437c(f)(1). COUNSEL SHALL MEET AND CONFER ON A PROPOSED ORDER AND PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

Attys. STEVE EGLER and STEVE BRUCE, Tel No. (415) 931-3070 of and appearing for Defendant People With Disabilities Foundation.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel is granted. Defendant will produce all documents responsive to requests nos. 1-9 within ten (10) days from the date of this order. To the extent that defendant is withholding any responsive document based on a claim of privilege, it will describe the document with sufficient particularity to enable plaintiff to challenge the claim. In addition, if defendants asserts that it is unable to produce a document based upon the order of the U.S. District Court it shall describe that document and explain why it contents are covered by that order. The complaint seeks money damages for an alleged breach of a contract to pay a bonus in legal fees based upon recoveries in the actions filed in the U.S. District Court. All of the requested documents relate to that claim and should have been produced without controversy. Defendant was not justified in refusing to produce any document without a protective order. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-17-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Atty. Rose-Ellen Heinz Fargrieve, Tel No. (415) 890-6057 appearing for Cross-Defendant D. Cameron Baker;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT D. CAMERON BAKER'S DEMURRER TO THE PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES FOUNDATION'S FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. As to the second cause of action for fraud, the First Amended Cross-Complaint (FACC) fails to sufficiently allege the elements of justifiable reliance and damages. The FACC also lacks the requisite specificity. (See Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, holding that the particularity requirement for fraud "necessitates pleading facts which show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered.") As to the third and fourth causes of action, the FACC fails to allege facts establishing that Cross-Complainant acted to its detriment because of the alleged duress and undue influence, in a way that can be reconciled with the statement in the Cross-Complaint that Cross-Complainant "refused" to give in to Cross-Defendant's alleged demands. (Cross-Complaint, par. 17.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. CROSS DEFENDANT D CAMERON BAKER'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. As to the third and fourth causes of action, Cross-Complainant is given leave to allege facts establishing that Cross-Complainant acted to its detriment because of the alleged duress and undue influence, in a way that can be reconciled with the statement in the Cross-Complaint that Cross-Complainant "refused" to give in to Cross-Defendant's alleged demands.
